rulings.Case Title: EST. OF DER HONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Need clarification on whether Ivy Fong died testate/intestate and also whether there has been any probate case of her estate. Petitions merely indicate "deceased" without said information. 2. Need information on whether Caroline Fong died testate/intestate, and her successor in interest needs to be identified. Petitions merely indicate "deceased" without said information. 3. Need further information on whether there has been any probate case of Hazel So's estate or trust in San Mateo County. If Sharon So-Eppercht was the court appointed executor, a certified copy of Letters will be needed before the final distribution as it appears Hazel So's shares from her parents and siblings' estates must be distributed to the estate of Hazel So. Prob. Code sec. 11802 and LRSF 14.35.G. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MAURICIO ANTONIO VARELA CARIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) ~$7,700 was spent during the accounting period for "groceries". Petitioner clarifies that "groceries" were not for food but for over the counter medication and household expenses. 2) ~$28,000 was spent on "Household Expenses", which items were insufficiently described. 3) ~$10,000 was spent on clothing for this accounting period. 4) ~$32,000 was spent on purchasing goods from Amazon (under the category of "Bookstores and Newsstands" and "Computer Networking Services and Digital Goods", neither of which appear to accurately describe the purchases). 5) Utilities are being paid solely by the Conservatee. 6) ~$2,000 was spent on "Miscellaneous Expenses" with inadequate descriptions. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORRAINE D. ELLIOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Pay The Fee Of The Administrators; Pay The Ordinary Attorneys' Fees And Costs; Distribute The Balance Of The Estate To The Heirs Of The Decedent And Discharge The Petitioner
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of the fourth amended petition has been filed. The ONLY CORRECT FORM OF NOTICE is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing. (2) Notice must include the instructions regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6. (3) In light of the removal of the former administrator, it appears that this fourth amended petition is now moot; the successor administrator will have to petition for final distribution after she is appointed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] There is no need to attach a printout of issues posted in the tentative ruling to a pleading; a fully amended petition should, by its very nature, address the issues.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORRAINE D. ELLIOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) No proof of service of the AMENDED petition e-filed on 5/9/24 has been filed. (2) The e-filing date was furthermore not at least 15 days before this hearing. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number is not for this petitioner. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIGUEL Q URIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/24. (No earlier availability.) 1. The "second supplement" filed on 5/1/24 again AMENDED the accounting. The petition caption is misleading. [See CRC 7.3 for definitions.] 2. No proof of service has been filed yet of the complete Notice of Hearing of the FIRST AND FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATOR, etc., as amended by the supplement or again by the second supplement. 3. Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please file proof of service (which is on the reverse side of Judicial Council form DE-120) that shows: (1) the full petition caption in item 1 on the Notice of Hearing, along with the full captain of the second supplement; (2) that complete notice was mailed directly to the interested parties at least 15 days before 7/8/24; and (3) that a copy of the instructions for remote appearance was attached to the notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/28/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEROY CHARLES COPE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. (Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Since the executor is also the trustee of the devisee trust and, as such, would have presumably waived any defect in notice, no notice was required. Bank statements are not required when no accounting is presented. The current values of securities should not be part of the proposed distribution, as those values change; only the number of shares of securities is required to be set forth.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCIS KUTTNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Regarding Decedents Apple Id
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/10/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/17/2024): 1) Does the custodian require specific findings or language to be included in the Court's order? If so, please provide such findings or language, to the extent not included in the prayers for relief and proposed order. 2) The petition requests an order confirming the petitioner's authority as the decedent's "agent" under the Stored Communications Act, but the decedent's personal representative does not qualify as the decedent's agent under 18 USC 2702(b)(1). See Ajemian v. Yahoo!, Inc. (2017) 84 N.E.3d 766, 772-773. 3) If the petitioner is requesting disclosure of the content of electronic communications, please provide a copy of the decedent's trust, power of attorney, or other record evidencing the decedent's consent to disclosure of the content of electronic communications required to be given to each custodian under Probate Code section 876(d). 4) No inventory and appraisal has been filed since the filing deadline on 02/13/2024. No extension has been sought under section 8800(b).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZELLA MAE BAILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The objection filed by Latoya Cunningham was not verified. See Probate Code section 1021(a)(2) & (b)(3). 2) The objection does not include any allegation challenging the petitioner's priority under section 8441(b) or his competency to serve as sole personal representative. No competing petition has been filed to seek appointment of the objector and her siblings as joint personal representatives. See section 8004(a). 3) The objection does not include any allegation contesting the will. No summons has been issued. See sections 8004(b) & 8250(a). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEROLD AND BARBARA W. BABIN TRUST U/D/T DATED DECEMBER 11, 1998 AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Modify Irrevocable Trust To Remove Mandatory Allocation Of Assets On Death Of First Spouse And Prohibition On Amendment
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Do the trustors' two children have any issue? Their interests would potentially be affected by this proposed modification. [Subparagraph 2 of paragraph B of Article SIXTH, now subparagraph 3 of the same pursuant to the First Amendment to the trust.] (2) Please address how the Court can disregard the provisions of paragraph C of Article ELEVENTH of the trust, given Probate Code Sections 15403(b). (3) Subparagraph 4 of paragraph D of Article FIFTH (Trust Two: The Family Share) appears to restrict the trustee's or co-trustee's power "to make any distribution of income to himself or herself or to participate in any decision to make distribution of income to himself or herself" of Trust Two..?? (4) The petitioner (who is the trustee) did not verify either supplement to her petition. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/28/24. (Not having courtesy copies makes reviewing pleadings challenging.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] Page 2 of the amendment to the trust and pp. 12 and 16 of the trust were also missing from the copies attached to the supplement filed on 4/8/24.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY HELLMAN BECHTLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/10/2024 (no earlier date available), as nothing has been filed to address the following issue (last updated and e-mailed to counsel on 04/24/2024): All trustees of the devisee trust must be listed as such at item 8 and must receive or waive notice. If the petitioner is the sole trustee, all beneficiaries entitled to notice under Probate Code section 1208(b) must be listed as such and must receive or waive notice. See LRSF 14.18(C)(2). Please file a supplement verified by the petitioner or his declaration clearly identifying the trustees (or trust beneficiaries if the petitioner is the sole trustee). A Judicial Council form cannot be used to supplement the petition. See CRC 7.104(b)(2).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK R. DENNES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the "supplement" to the petition was actually an AMENDMENT correcting the addresses of two of the heirs. The original notice was apparently correct; the petition was incorrect. Status of closing the estate will be set for 6/9/2025. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 6/9/2025 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNESTINE PHILOMENA BURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was e-filed on 5/3/24. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends.[CRC 7.53(a).] (2) Both the mailed and published notice must clearly indicate that an AMENDED petition has been filed. (3) The published notice does not indicate that the petitioner requests the will to be admitted. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
